channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: Yukon government projects $82M deficit amid debt constraints and infrastructure priorities
item.description: Yukon Premier Currie Dixon introduced the territory's 2026-27 budget, highlighting a projected $82-million deficit, which he described as a record for Yukon. He attributed the deficit to 'unsustainabl...

item.title: Impact of Magufuli's Policies on Tanzania’s Property and Urban Development
item.description: The article reflects on the legacy of the late President John Pombe Magufuli, five years after his passing on March 17, 2021. Magufuli’s leadership was characterised by discipline, accountability, and...

item.title: Johannesburg Metro Police Demolish Informal Homes in Ennerdale Amid Housing Delays
item.description: In Ennerdale Extension 7, Johannesburg, dozens of families have been left homeless after the Johannesburg Metropolitan Police Department (JMPD) demolished around 50 informal structures on 16 March 202...

item.title: Escalating Middle East Energy Attacks Threaten Global Oil and Gas Supplies
item.description: The Guardian reports on escalating military strikes targeting vital oil and gas infrastructure across the Middle East, triggering fears of severe disruption to global energy supplies. Following an Isr...

item.title: Study Finds Social Media Linked to Lower Happiness Levels
item.description: A recent study highlighted in the World Happiness Report suggests that social media usage is contributing to lower levels of happiness among users worldwide. According to the report, frequent engageme...

item.title: UC Berkeley Settles Antisemitism Lawsuit, Revises Policies on Zionist Speakers
item.description: UC Berkeley has reached a $1 million settlement to resolve a lawsuit filed by two Jewish groups over alleged antisemitic incidents following the 2023 Hamas-Israel conflict. The settlement mandates tha...

item.title: Rajnath Singh calls for self-reliant drone manufacturing ecosystem to strengthen India’s defence capabilities
item.description: Defence Minister Rajnath Singh has emphasised the urgent need for India to develop a strong indigenous drone production ecosystem to ensure strategic autonomy and enhance national security. Speaking a...

item.title: Xanadu Quantum Technologies prepares for IPO amid ambitious growth plans
item.description: Christian Weedbrook, founder and CEO of Xanadu Quantum Technologies Inc., has transformed from a film school dropout into a leading figure in quantum computing. Based in Toronto, Xanadu is poised to g...

item.title: Prime Video 2026 lineup: New shows, films and seasons for Hindi, Tamil and Telugu audiences
item.description: Prime Video has unveiled its extensive 2026 slate, signalling continued investment in Indian content despite industry-wide uncertainties. The platform will introduce new shows across Hindi, Tamil, and...

item.title: Allegations of child sexual exploitation linked to West Midlands mini-marts over past decade
item.description: A BBC investigation has uncovered serious allegations of child sexual exploitation (CSE) connected to convenience stores across the West Midlands, particularly in Dudley. According to internal Dudley ...

item.title: Raphael Tuju Appeals to IG Kanja Over Police Occupation of Dari Business Park
item.description: Former Cabinet Secretary Raphael Tuju has written formally to Inspector General of Police Japheth Koome, protesting the ongoing police occupation of Dari Business Park in Karen, Nairobi. According to ...

item.title: Jan Bergman: From Toekomsrus Streets to Boxing Glory
item.description: In this compelling biography, Thabile Mange chronicles the life of retired boxing champion Jan Bergman, not merely as a story of sporting success, but as a lens on South Africa's turbulent transition ...

item.title: Nairobi City Thunder Qualifies for 2026 Basketball Africa League
item.description: Nairobi City Thunder has made waves in Kenyan basketball by securing back-to-back qualifications for the Basketball Africa League (BAL). The team clinched the East Division Elite 16 title for the seco...

item.title: Master Hanz Cua shares daily feng shui insights for March 20, 2026
item.description: The article published by ABS-CBN Lifestyle features the daily feng shui forecast shared by Master Hanz Cua for March 20, 2026. Known as a leading feng shui expert in the Philippines, Master Hanz offer...

item.title: 4chan Refuses to Pay UK Online Safety Fine, Citing First Amendment Protections
item.description: The UK’s media regulator, Ofcom, has fined the online message board 4chan roughly $700,000 (about £520,000) for failing to comply with the UK’s Online Safety Act. The penalty includes $65,000 for not ...

item.title: Meningitis vaccination expanded in Kent after outbreak linked to nightclub
item.description: The meningitis B (MenB) vaccination campaign in Kent has been widened after a deadly outbreak linked to Club Chemistry in Canterbury. Initially limited to University of Kent students living in halls, ...

item.title: Kenya Seeks Sh400m to Support Evacuation of Citizens from Middle East and Russia Amid Escalating Conflicts
item.description: The State Department for Diaspora Affairs has asked Parliament to allocate Sh400 million to help repatriate Kenyans trapped in the Middle East and Russia due to ongoing conflicts. Ambassador Hellen Gi...

item.title: Pakistan Telecom Firms Launch 5G Services in Major Cities After Spectrum Auction
item.description: Telecommunication companies in Pakistan have officially launched 5G services in selected cities following the awarding of 5G spectrum licences in Islamabad. The recent government spectrum auction sold...

item.title: Car Accident in Vadodara Causes Biker to Fall Ten Feet
item.description: A shocking road accident took place in Vadodara, Gujarat, when a car collided with a two-wheeler, causing the biker to be thrown approximately ten feet away from the point of impact. The incident was ...

item.title: Ndume Urges Tinubu to Focus on Security and Welfare Over Political Defections
item.description: Senator Ali Ndume, wey dey represent Borno South, don advise President Bola Tinubu make e no allow politicians wey dey decamp to APC distract am from more serious national issues. Ndume talk this one ...
